slides.kicker: // sample template · publishing and print media
slides.h1:
From Press to Pixel
the modern newsroom.
slides.lead: Demo presentation of this template. All text is sample (dummy) content and exists only to show the styles.
slides.notes: Opening slide. Holds the event title, a one-line subtitle and a meta row at the bottom. Highlight one word with em for the accent color.

slides.lead: Five roles, one shared front page.
slides.notes: Team slide. Uses initials in a colored circle instead of photos, so it needs no images.

slides.lead: Three parts, with room for questions between them.
slides.notes: Agenda as three large tiles. Good at the start of a block.

slides.lead: Readership, revenue and the biggest bottlenecks.
slides.notes: Divider slide between blocks. Large headline on a colored gradient.

slides.lead: Attention now spreads across print, web, app and inbox. Value comes from meeting the reader on every surface.
slides.footnote: // illustrative slide with an enlarged image
slides.notes: Hero slide: big headline, bold illustration, short caption. Good for a key point or a chapter.

items: growth in digital subscriptions year over year
saved daily on manual page layout
bullets: Measure loyalty, not just reach: renewals beat first clicks.
Start where the audience already is and the pain is loudest.
A small pilot with one clear metric beats a grand plan without one.
slides.notes: KPI dashboard: three cards with an icon, a large number and a label, supporting bullets below.

slides.lead: The model does not chase clicks, it earns renewals.
bullets: Free headlines and sharp newsletters draw new readers.
A timely paywall turns a habit into a subscription.
Daily relevance keeps the reader opening the app.
Smooth billing and value keep churn low.
slides.footnote: // two column slide with an illustration
slides.notes: Two column slide: bullets with a strong lead word on the left, illustration on the right. Text scales to fit.

slides.lead: Same story, two business models and two revenue curves.
cards: Free pages paid by advertising. Volatile and crowded.
Some free articles, then a paywall. A gentle on ramp.
Readers pay for depth. Steady and predictable.
Premium reporting paywalled, breaking news open.
slides.notes: A 2x2 grid of cards. Good for comparison or for listing variants.

slides.lead: Six typical building blocks of a modern newsroom.
cards: One desk for writing, editing and scheduling.
Plan print pages and digital drops in one view.
Live dashboards of reach, dwell and renewals.
Convert engaged readers into paying subscribers.
Push to web, app, print and inbox at once.
Surface the right story to the right reader.
slides.notes: A 3x2 grid with an icon in each card header. Great for a tool or capability overview.

items: Growth slows, so it pays to harden the basics.
Digital habits shift fast and reshape the front page.
Paper volumes fall and budgets follow the readers.
slides.notes: Numbered cards with small trend charts and an illustration on the right. Ideal for three possible futures.

slides.lead: Five steps, each with a clear output.
slides.notes: A horizontal flow of steps with arrows. Each box has a title and a small caption.

slides.quote: The deadline never moved, only the press did. Whoever turns a story around fastest still owns the morning.
slides.by: Sample quote to show the style
slides.notes: Quote slide with a bold left rule. Keep the attribution small.

slides.lead: Simple, but easy to forget under deadline.
bullets: Every section has a named editor who answers for it.
You know in advance how you will read success.
On sensitive stories a person always decides.
slides.notes: A plain bullet slide with a strong lead word. Text grows automatically to fill the space.

slides.lead: A scoop without an owner and a metric ends up forgotten.
slides.notes: Statement slide: one large sentence centered on the page. Use it for the key message.

slides.lead: What to do this week, this month and this quarter.
slides.notes: Second divider, opening the practical part.

slides.lead: We switch into the tool and lay out tomorrow's edition live.
slides.footnote: // placeholder slide for a demo or a break
slides.notes: Placeholder slide. Centered headline, ideal for a live demo, a break or questions.
Closing slide in the style of the title. Contact and call to action.
